checks.a_analysis: The output content closely follows the text found in the source document. While the title and image alt text are AI-generated, these are within the scope of acceptable changes. No hallucinated body text or invented agenda items were detected.
The physical addresses for the meeting locations (3514 Bush Street and 109 East North Street) were verified against the source and are correct. No phone numbers or URLs were present to check.
Both pages of the agenda are represented in the output. The high-level agenda items (1-16) and the additional sections regarding committee meetings are present.

The source document uses a tabular format with three columns (Tab, Topic, Presenter) for approximately 18 items. The output correctly identifies the table headers but only produces a single row containing those headers. The actual data that should have populated the table rows was instead moved to a list format elsewhere, effectively leaving the table itself with zero data rows.
checks.b_evidence_source: The source contains a table structure for all agenda items (Call to Order through Item 16) with columns for Tab, Topic, and Presenter.
checks.b_evidence_output: The JSON table element at reading_order 3 contains only the header row: [Tab, Topic, Presenter]. It contains no data rows.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: There is a severe loss of data within the agenda items. First, all sub-items (a, b, c, etc.) for items 1, 5, 6, and 11 are missing. This includes essential financial data (fund balances and disbursements) for the NG 911, CMRS, PSAP, and Grant funds. Second, the entire 'Presenter' column from the source is omitted; none of the names (e.g., Eric Boyette, Pokey Harris, Tina Gardner, Richard Bradford) appear in the output text for their respective topics. This constitutes a loss of well over 25% of the document's informative text content.
checks.b_evidence_source: Item 5 includes sub-items a-f with values like 'June 2019 Fund Balance $ 44,749,222'. Item 6 includes sub-items a-e. Presenters are listed for every item (e.g., 'Eric Boyette (01)').
checks.b_evidence_output: Item 5 in the output is only: '5. Consent Agenda (Vote Required)'. Item 6 is only: '6. Executive Director Report'. No presenter names are included in any list items.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The identified errors are significant omissions of entire columns and sub-sections rather than minor wording changes. Therefore, this check is marked as PASS as the issues are covered under C4 and C5.

checks.a_analysis: The output does not contain duplicated text blocks. Each section and list item from the source appears only once in the corresponding JSON elements. The logos appearing twice is consistent with their presence on both pages of the source PDF.
The heading hierarchy is logical and consistent. H1 is used for the main title 'AGENDA', and H2 is used for major sections like meeting details and committee meetings. No levels are skipped.

The main agenda items are presented in a 3-column layout (Tab, Topic, Presenter) in the source PDF. In the output, only the header row is captured as a table (reading_order 3). The body of this table is incorrectly converted into a simple list (reading_order 4 and 7), which merges the 'Tab' column into the 'Topic' text and entirely omits the 'Presenter' column and various financial sub-rows.
checks.b_evidence_source: The main agenda table on page 1 includes columns for 'Tab', 'Topic', and 'Presenter'. Item 5 includes presenter 'Pokey Harris (03)' and sub-rows with financial figures.
checks.b_evidence_output: The output captures the headers in a table at reading_order 3, but the corresponding data rows are converted to a list at reading_order 4, which omits the 'Presenter' data entirely and excludes sub-rows.

checks.a_analysis: The reading order generally follows the logical flow of the document. While the branding logo for page 1 (reading_order 6) is placed after the main content of page 1, this is a standard adjustment for accessibility. The core sequence of agenda items 1-16 is preserved correctly.

The source document contains significant nested list structures for agenda items 1, 5, 6, 11, and 15. The output fails to preserve this nesting; the 'nested_list' field is null for these items, and the sub-items themselves (e.g., items 5a-5f and their associated financial values) are omitted from the output.
checks.b_evidence_source: Item 5 'Consent Agenda (Vote Required)' contains a nested list 'a)' through 'f)' with specific financial data for funds.
checks.b_evidence_output: The list item for '5. Consent Agenda (Vote Required)' has 'nested_list': null and does not contain the sub-items.

checks.a_analysis: No links were identified in the source document, and none were incorrectly injected into the output.

checks.a_analysis: The source document presents the agenda in a structured three-column layout (Tab, Topic, Presenter) with nested sub-items (e.g., items 5a-5f and 6a-6e). The HTML output significantly simplifies this layout into a flat bulleted list. Most critically, the information from the 'Presenter' column and all the indented sub-items (including detailed financial figures and migration statuses) are completely missing from the visual output. This is a major failure to represent the visual organization and hierarchy of the source document.
checks.b_evidence_source: The agenda items on pages 1 and 2 are organized in a three-column layout (Tab, Topic, Presenter) with indented sub-sections (e.g., Item 5 has sub-items a-f and Item 6 has a-e).
checks.b_evidence_output: The HTML output renders the agenda items as a simple flat bulleted list. The 'Presenter' column and all indented sub-items (e.g., financial data in 5b-e) are missing from the visual rendering.
checks.b_evidence_location: Main agenda body, Items 1 through 16, pages 1-2.

checks.a_analysis: The agenda in the source document is visually structured as a table with column headers. In the HTML, the headers ('Tab', 'Topic', 'Presenter') are rendered as a separate block, and the content below them is a bulleted list that loses the column alignment and omits the 'Presenter' data entirely. Furthermore, the financial data in Item 5 (NG 911 Fund, CMRS Fund, etc.), which is structured as a two-column financial table in the PDF, is completely absent from the HTML rendering.
checks.b_evidence_source: The agenda uses a three-column structure with headers 'Tab', 'Topic', 'Presenter'. Item 5 includes structured financial tables with fund balances and disbursements.
checks.b_evidence_output: The column headers are rendered as a text block separate from the list content. The presenter column information is missing from the list. The financial tables for the various funds in Item 5 are missing entirely.
checks.b_evidence_location: Agenda table structure and Item 5 financial data, page 1.

checks.a_analysis: The meaningful images from the source document (the NC DIT 911 Board logos) are correctly present in the HTML output. One logo is placed between the items corresponding to the page break, and another is placed at the end of the document, which is a reasonable rendering for a continuous HTML page.

There is no visual duplication of body content in the HTML output. While document titles and section headings appear in both the navigation sidebar (TOC) and the body of the document, this is expected accessible design and not a duplication defect. The agenda items and meeting notes appear only once in the document body.
